project.name: Ableton Project Setup
project.description: Automated Ableton Live project builder — takes raw stems, classifies them, detects BPM, and creates a fully laid-out .als file matching Sam's mixing workflow.
project.location: Wired Masters Dropbox/Sam Wills/0.1---GIT HUB---/Ableton Project Setup/

Source/project_builder.py.purpose: Main orchestrator: scans stem folders, classifies stems, detects BPM, copies audio, builds flat references, detects group buses, and patches the final ALS.
Source/project_builder.py.notes: Contains single-version and multi-version build paths. As of 2026-06-26 it can call audio_ml_classify.py for filename-unknown stems. Multi-version later stacks are placed on phrase slots, then nudged together by the earliest credible kick-named layer.

Source/stem_classifier.py.purpose: Filename-based classification and display track-name generation.
Source/stem_classifier.py.notes: Patterns cover drums, bass, music, vocals, FX, sends, references, and common messy export names.

Source/audio_ml_classify.py.purpose: Heavy audio-content classifier for unnamed stems using Demucs source bins and optional Whisper vocal confirmation.
Source/audio_ml_classify.py.notes: Used for the Moby unnamed-stem test. Runs as a subprocess from project_builder.py so ML failures do not abort the build.

Source/stem_analysis.py.purpose: Numpy audio analysis for full-mix/reference detection and group-bus/submix detection.
Source/stem_analysis.py.notes: Keeps full mixes and detected buses out of the flat-reference sum.

Source/als_patcher.py.purpose: Raw-text ALS patching engine for tracks, clips, groups, locators, routing, colours, and regions.
Source/als_patcher.py.notes: Do not replace with XML libraries; Ableton is sensitive to formatting.

Source/bpm_detector.py.purpose: Pure-stdlib BPM detection from kick/drum onsets.
Source/bpm_detector.py.notes: Returns both folded grid phase and first_actual_onset_sec so multi-version alignment can use the physical timing anchor.

Source/bounce.py.purpose: Flat-reference WAV summing with numpy fast path and stdlib fallback.
Source/bounce.py.notes: Used to print one red FLAT REF track rather than duplicating stems into a reference group.

Source/versions.py.purpose: Detects alternate-version subfolders by mirrored element keys.
Source/versions.py.notes: Same-folder name-token versions are not implemented yet.

Source/validate_project.py.purpose: Canonical generated-project validator for ALS gzip/XML readability, tempo, tempo automation, Session Time, refs, FLAT REF, and file refs.
Source/validate_project.py.notes: Use this instead of verify_output.py for arbitrary outputs.

Config/project_builder.json.purpose: Local defaults for template path, output base, ML interpreter, and ML enablement.

sessions.summary: Three features: (1) pure-stdlib BPM auto-detection (Source/bpm_detector.py) borrowed from Automated DJ Mixes (attack_onsets + lattice_fit), no numpy/scipy/Demucs; bpm arg now optional, detects from kick; validated exact on 4 kicks. (2) Clip naming — track=display, clip=original filename. (3) Classifier patterns BVs/Tops/Fills/CABASA/DRM_/Ref Bounce. (4) Replaced duplicated-stems flat-reference GroupTrack with a single bounced flat-ref track (Source/bounce.py, pure-stdlib stem sum -> float32 WAV): color 37, muted, Ext. Out, last; supplied ref/master files kept as separate match tracks, never summed. Retired the ref-group-expand bug. (5) Working-track grouping: drums/music/vocals/fx with 2+ stems wrapped in a GroupTrack (audible, Main, expanded, category-coloured; children -> AudioOut/GroupTrack + TrackGroupId); kick/bass/sends standalone. (6) bounce.py numpy fast path (~15x) with bit-identical pure-stdlib fallback.

sessions.summary: First real production run: built Replicage - Amen [Sound Better] from 41 desktop stems into 2. Ongoing Stem Mixes (single-version; the Radio/Extended/Dub arrangements are baked end-to-end into one long timeline per stem, so single-version is correct). Sam happy, ~30 min saved. Then shipped two stem-handling features per Sam's spec: (1) wet/dry VOCALS-ONLY + explicit WET+DRY pairs only — find_dry_stems requires a wet-token sibling sharing base identity (export index + wet/dry tokens stripped), build_project passes only vocal stems; the dry copy is parked in a muted, collapsed grey Dry group (color 37), out of the flat-ref sum; a music ARP DRY beside a plain ARP is left untouched. (2) Empty/silent stems (peak window RMS < SILENCE_FLOOR_DB -60 dBFS via find_audio_regions(return_peak=True)) moved to the very bottom with their own colour (SILENT_TRACK_COLOR 12), out of layout + sum. als_patcher group runs now carry per-run muted/unfolded/group_color. Decision recorded: BPM always rounded (whole or half), never warp. Validated: test_wet_dry 7/7, combined synthetic e2e inspected in ALS, validate_project PASS, full suite green.

decisions.decision: ALS files are patched as raw text lines, not via XML libraries.
decisions.why: Ableton's parser is strict about whitespace, attribute order, and line endings. xml.etree.ElementTree corrupts the format. See ABLETON_INTERACTION.md.

decisions.decision: Stems are placed unwarped (IsWarped=false). BPM detected from kick/snare sets global tempo.
decisions.why: Sam's stems arrive pre-rendered at the correct BPM. No time-stretching needed.

decisions.decision: Flat reference is always our own bounce (sum of mix stems), never a supplied 'ref'/'riff'/master file. Single track at the bottom, color 14 (red), muted, routed to Ext. Out. Supplied references kept as separate match tracks (also red). No GroupTrack.
decisions.why: A supplied ref file can't be trusted to equal the stem sum (may be another artist's track or a limited master); verifying it would require bouncing the sum anyway, so always print our own. Single track + Ext. Out bypasses the master chain and retires the long-standing 'ref group opens expanded' bug.

decisions.decision: Group-bus / sub-mix stems (a bus left among individual stems) are detected via find_group_buses() in stem_analysis.py: stems are sample-aligned, so a bus is the sum of its members; a non-negative greedy matching pursuit (Gram-matrix space) flags any stem reconstructed >88% by a positive sum of >=2 others. Buses are parked muted/grey (37) at the very bottom below refs, kept for recall, and excluded from the flat-ref sum. Non-negativity flags the bus, never its members.
decisions.why: Sam: this is a big, frequent problem on real packs — a producer leaves a group bus among the stems, you press play and everything sounds wrong (double-counted), then have to hunt for which stem is the bus. Filenames/spectra can't distinguish a bus from a stem, but the sample-aligned sum relationship can. Validated: caught Coldabank 'All Drums' + Far Away 'BASS EGYBE' (=b+b_3) with no false positives.

decisions.decision: Ambiguous stems get an audio-content check (Source/stem_analysis.py, numpy FFT): a file filenames can't place that reads as a full mix (crest<=5 + 6+ bands + sustain>=0.6) is moved to references so it's never summed into the flat bounce. Approach borrowed from Loudness Leveller (band energy) + Automated DJ Mixes (low-end) but implemented numpy-only (no librosa/ffmpeg); filename-only fallback if numpy absent. Audio kick-detection was tried and dropped — kick vs bass overlap too much on aggregate spectral features; filenames (incl. 'K') handle kicks.
decisions.why: A test batch of 5 random finished mixes showed cryptically-named full mixes ('Current', 'TEST MIX') being summed into the flat ref and polluting it (Far Away peaked 3.85). Filenames are unreliable on messy real packs; audio content is the robust signal. Crest factor (compression) is the key full-mix discriminator, tuned/validated on labelled real files.

decisions.decision: bounce.py uses numpy when installed (optional import) with a pure-stdlib fallback; both paths verified bit-identical. Working groups coloured with the category colour; group names Drums/Bass/Music/Vox/FX (bass IS grouped per Sam 2026-06-25). Reference tracks coloured red (14). Silence trim retuned tighter (headroom 55dB / window 0.1s / min-gap 2.5s / tail 1s) so clips hug audio without chopping decays.
decisions.why: Optional numpy keeps the tool install-free yet ~15x faster where numpy exists. Category-coloured group headers read at a glance. Sam asked for red refs, bass grouping, and tighter silence trimming after seeing the first demo.

decisions.decision: Filename-unknown stems can be classified with an optional heavy ML second stage: Demucs energy bins map to drums/bass/music/vocals and Whisper confirms likely vocals.
decisions.why: The Moby test pack intentionally used generic names such as Audio 10_02.wav. Filename rules alone would collapse most tracks into Music; source-bin classification gives a better first-pass project while preserving a human-readable report for review.

decisions.decision: Multi-version later sections are placed as a whole stack on the next phrase slot, then the entire stack is nudged together using the earliest credible kick-named layer as the grid anchor.
decisions.why: Radio edits can have no dry kick at the start. Fallon proved that anchoring to the dry kick or to the locator is wrong; the musical stack must stay locked together and only receive the small left/right nudge Sam would do by eye in Ableton.
